output:
# From FLATFIELD to CONTOUR: Neighbourhood-Conditional Bandwidth and Calibration Fields for Exact-Collapse In-Context Text Classification

## 1. Background and Current State of the Art

Large language models (LLMs) are pre-trained on large corpora to capture semantics in a task-independent way. Pre-training is either a masked language model (MLM) that predicts randomly masked tokens (BERT [3], RoBERTa [4], BART [5]), or an auto-regressive / causal language model (CLM) that predicts a token only from its predecessors (GPT-2 [6], LLaMA [7], Mistral [8]). When scaled from millions to billions of parameters and instruction-tuned [1], [2], these models adapt to a broad set of tasks through small changes in the context supplied as input (the prompt). In few-shot or in-context learning (ICL), a small number of labelled examples is appended to a prompt instruction to steer generation for a downstream task, with no gradient update to the model [1].

A common and important use of ICL is the PREDICTIVE task of TEXT CLASSIFICATION. Each class is specified by a set of label words called a VERBALISER [9]: for binary sentiment, the positive class might be {good, great, wonderful}. The frozen decoder scores those label words conditioned on the demonstrations, and the prediction is the argmax over the resulting verbaliser posterior; prompt-engineering variants such as PET [9] and LM-BFF [11] elaborate the template but keep one global verbaliser. ICL is thus a non-parametric procedure layered on a FROZEN model: the prompt, not the weights, carries the task.

This makes the ICL-as-kNN analogy natural. Like k-nearest-neighbours, an ICL prediction depends on the LOCAL TOPOLOGY: a localised set of similar instances and their labels (the few-shot demonstrations / retrieved neighbours), aggregated into a soft vote. The only structural difference from k-NN is that the aggregation runs through a FROZEN set of encoder-decoder parameters rather than an explicit distance kernel. This analogy has driven two complementary lines of work that constitute today's state of the art.

The first is the CALIBRATION lineage, which targets ICL's documented decision biases - majority-label, recency, and common-token bias - and its brittleness to demonstration choice and order. Contextual calibration fits a single affine map from content-free inputs to debias the posterior [12]; order-sensitivity studies show large variance across permutations [15]; "rethinking demonstrations" shows the label mapping itself can matter less than the input distribution [13]; label-bias calibration estimates a corpus-level prior shift [14]; prototypical calibration fits one decision boundary in feature space [21]; and surface-form competition [22] and noisy-channel prompting [20] reparametrise the scoring rule. Every one of these corrections is a SINGLE global object applied uniformly to all inputs.

The second is the RETRIEVAL / kNN-ICL lineage, which reads the local topology explicitly. KATE retrieves the nearest demonstrations to fill the prompt [16]; EPR learns a retriever to select prompts [17]; kNN-Prompting casts a nearest-neighbour vote over LLM states with calibration-free inference [18]; and kNN-LM interpolates a nearest-neighbour distribution into a frozen language model with one global weight [19]. These methods spend the local signal on SELECTING demonstrations or on a GLOBALLY-weighted vote, with a single bandwidth / interpolation weight shared by every query.

What all of this leaves open is the same thing on both sides: the correction is GLOBAL. The calibration lineage applies one input-independent affine map; the retrieval lineage uses one global bandwidth or one global interpolation weight, even though it reads a local signal. Neither expresses a correction that VARIES across the local topology - per-neighbourhood bias and per-neighbourhood effective bandwidth. That open gap is what this proposal names and closes.

## 2. Key Findings and Analysis

We name the open gap FLATFIELD - the "flat-field aggregation limit." Vanilla verbaliser-based ICL maps the frozen decoder's verbaliser posterior to a decision using TWO global, input-independent mechanisms: (i) a single implicit similarity bandwidth (one effective neighbourhood size / temperature applied to every query through the frozen attention kernel), and (ii) at most one global affine calibration of the posterior, as in contextual calibration [12]. Both are CONSTANT across the input space - a flat field. The heterogeneous phenomenon they cannot express is that the optimal decision correction is a FIELD over the input space, not a constant: ICL decision bias and the optimal effective-k vary across the local topology of the demonstration manifold - dense versus sparse regions, near versus far from a class boundary, neighbourhoods whose label mix induces different majority-label bias. Formally, the best constant correction b_glob = argmin_b E_x[loss(x; b)] is a single point, whereas the heterogeneous optimum b*(x) is a function of x; the baseline hypothesis class {constant b} cannot contain b*(.) whenever Var_x[b*(x)] > 0. The gap is representational, not a tuning failure [provable-by-construction].

Critically, this loss is SILENT under pooled accuracy. A global calibration is by definition the INPUT-AVERAGED optimum, so the residual heterogeneous bias r(x) = b*(x) - b_glob has near-zero mean over the pool by construction and CANCELS in pool-averaged accuracy, while remaining large and decision-flipping inside specific neighbourhoods. A metric that averages over the input space is structurally blind to a defect whose signature is zero-mean-but-high-variance over that same space - which is why this loss has largely escaped study as an object in its own right. The loss SCALES with the cross-neighbourhood variance of the optimal local correction, Var_x[b*(x)], observable through proxies such as the dispersion of per-neighbourhood majority-label bias, local density variation (dispersion of kNN distances), and distance-to-boundary spread. It VANISHES exactly when the field is flat - uniform density, uniform neighbourhood bias, b*(x) = b_glob everywhere - at which point any correct method must reduce to vanilla ICL.

Our method, CONTOUR (Conditional Neighbourhood-TOpology Re-weighting and recalibration), supplies the missing capability at the right layer: the LLM stays FROZEN and adaptation happens at the verbaliser-posterior aggregation layer, at inference time, with no fine-tuning. CONTOUR replaces the two flat fields with two ORTHOGONAL, independently gated, locally-varying fields. The combined score is f_c(x) = s_c(x) + g_cal * b_c(x) + g_knn * lambda(x) * log pi_c^kNN(x), where s_c(x) = aggregate_{v in V(c)} log p_LLM(v | prompt(x), x) is the frozen verbaliser score. KNOB 1 is a local affine CALIBRATION field b_c(x) = -log p_LLM(c | contentfree, prompt_local(N_k(x))), shrunk toward the global estimate via a reliability gate, b_c(x) = (1 - tau(x)) b_c^glob + tau(x) b_c^local(x) [designed]. KNOB 2 is a local BANDWIDTH / kNN-evidence field with kernel w_i(x) = exp(-d_i / H(x)), self-tuned bandwidth H(x) = c0 * median_i(d_i), neighbour vote pi_c^kNN(x) = (sum_i w_i 1[y_i = c]) / (sum_i w_i), and density-reliability blend lambda(x) [designed]. Each knob reads the per-query kNN local topology (distances d_i give local density; neighbour labels y_i give local bias).

CONTOUR carries an EXACT-COLLAPSE identity [provable-by-construction]: at the zero gate g_cal = 0 and g_knn = 0, both added terms are exactly 0.0, so f_c(x) = s_c(x) bitwise for every x and c, and yhat_CONTOUR(x) = yhat_vanilla(x) decision-for-decision. The collapse error E_collapse = max over a fixed eval batch of max_c |f_c(x) - s_c(x)| equals 0 and is asserted <= 1e-6 by a CI gate every build. This is a do-no-harm spine: enabling the machinery can only ADD corrections the baseline misses, never perturb the baseline decision when gates are off. The contribution is the four-way novelty wedge C1-C4, satisfied by no prior family: C1 operates at the frozen-LLM inference / verbaliser-posterior layer with no fine-tuning; C2 conditions on an explicit observable local signal (per-query kNN density + neighbour labels); C3 models the specific heterogeneity the baseline cannot - a per-neighbourhood FIELD of (bandwidth, affine calibration); C4 provides provable exact collapse to vanilla ICL. Every near-miss family satisfies AT MOST TWO clauses: vanilla ICL (C1, C4), contextual calibration [12] (C1, C4), KATE [16] / EPR [17] / kNN-Prompting [18] / kNN-LM [19] (C1, C2), noisy-channel [20] / surface-form [22] (C1), PET [9] / LM-BFF [11] (none, they fine-tune or change the template). The conjunction C1 AND C2 AND C3 AND C4 is unclaimed, and named prior methods are recovered as degenerate corners of CONTOUR's field (Section 3, Question 4).

## 3. Novel Research Idea (5-Question Formulation)

**Here is a high-level summarized insight of a research field Machine Learning.**
**Here are the five core questions:**

**[Question 1] - What is the problem?**

Can replacing vanilla verbaliser-based in-context learning's two global, input-independent mechanisms (one fixed similarity bandwidth and one global posterior calibration) with CONTOUR, a frozen-LLM inference-time field of neighbourhood-conditional bandwidth and affine verbaliser-posterior calibration estimated from each query's kNN local topology (local density and local bias), improve text-classification accuracy in neighbourhoods with heterogeneous local bias or density while provably collapsing decision-for-decision to vanilla ICL at the zero-gate setting with E_collapse <= 1e-6?

**[Question 2] - Why is it interesting and important?**

P1 (the blind spot is broad because the deployment mode is dominant). ICL is the dominant few-shot deployment mode for frozen LLMs: a verbaliser maps each class to label words, the frozen decoder scores them on a handful of demonstrations, and argmax over the verbaliser posterior is the prediction [1], [9], [11]. It is attractive precisely because it ships with NO gradient update and runs on any frozen backbone, from encoder scorers (BERT [3], RoBERTa [4], BART [5]) to decoder LLMs (GPT-2 [6], LLaMA [7], Mistral [8]). But the very thing that makes it cheap - one frozen forward pass mapped through one fixed verbaliser-posterior aggregation - is where FLATFIELD lives: vanilla ICL applies ONE global bandwidth and at most ONE global calibration, both constant across the input space [12]. Because this pipeline is shared by essentially every frozen-LLM classifier, a structural limitation at that layer is a BROAD latent issue, not a corner case.

P2 (the failure is SILENT under aggregate accuracy, and it is exactly the documented ICL pathology). The known biases of ICL - majority-label, recency, common-token - and brittleness to demonstration choice and order [12], [13], [14], [15] are the symptoms of a correction that SHOULD vary across the input but is forced to be constant. Contextual calibration fixes only the INPUT-AVERAGED component with a single affine map [12]; by construction that map minimises the pooled loss, so the residual per-neighbourhood bias r(x) = b*(x) - b_glob has near-zero pool mean and CANCELS in pool-averaged accuracy. The heterogeneous loss is therefore SILENT: aggregate accuracy can look healthy while error concentrates and flips decisions inside specific neighbourhoods - which is why this loss has escaped study as an object in its own right.

P3 (fairness / disparate-impact angle). A zero-mean-over-the-pool residual is not zero-mean over SUBPOPULATIONS. If b*(x) is systematically off in a region of the manifold - a topic cluster, a dialect or register, a low-density subgroup - then per-neighbourhood miscalibration concentrates error on that subgroup while pooled accuracy, dominated by the majority regions, hides it. The same majority-label and density effects documented at corpus level [12], [14] become disparate-impact effects at the neighbourhood level, so a correction conditional on LOCAL topology is both an accuracy lever and a fairness lever.

P4 (deployability: ship-in-shadow with provable zero regression). CONTOUR is frozen-LLM, inference-time, added at the verbaliser-posterior layer; it needs no retraining and composes with any backbone [1], [3]-[8]. Its exact-collapse identity (E_collapse <= 1e-6 at g_cal=0, g_knn=0 [provable-by-construction]) means it can ship in shadow with a CI-gated guarantee of zero decision-level regression when gates are off: enabling the machinery can only ADD corrections. This is a materially different deployment story from methods that edit the prompt, re-weight the vote globally, or fine-tune - those change baseline behaviour unconditionally and cannot offer a strict, machine-checkable safe-generalisation contract.

P5 (generality: an operational lens, not a one-off trick). The ICL-as-kNN analogy - retrieved demonstrations behave like neighbours, the verbaliser posterior like a soft local vote [16], [17], [18], [19] - has so far been used mainly as an intuition for SELECTING demonstrations. CONTOUR turns it into an OPERATIONAL, calibratable correction: it reads the per-query kNN local topology (local density from distances, local bias from labels) for free and builds a per-neighbourhood FIELD of (bandwidth, affine calibration). This "covariate-conditional correction over the local topology" lens generalises to any verbaliser-based predictive ICL setting that exposes a retrieval index, so the contribution is a reusable principle, not a benchmark-specific patch.

**[Question 3] - Why is it hard?**

The naive reading - "just do a local kNN vote and a local calibration per query" - fails on SEVEN independent counts. Each is non-incidental (it does not vanish with more compute or a bigger model), and the difficulty is their CONJUNCTION: defeating any six still leaves the method broken unless the seventh is handled. Each obstacle maps one-to-one onto a Q5 ablation rung.

Obstacle 1 - High-variance local estimator under small k [designed]. b_c(x) and pi_c^kNN(x) are estimated from only k neighbours (k typically 8..64); a per-query estimate has variance O(1/k_eff) with effective sample size k_eff = (sum w_i)^2 / sum w_i^2, and for peaked kernels k_eff << k. A naive fully-local calibration OVERFITS and is provably expected to be WORSE than the global constant whenever noise variance exceeds the signal Var_x[b*(x)] (bias-variance crossover). Design answer: shrink toward global via tau(x) (James-Stein / empirical-Bayes [24], [25]) gated by neighbourhood reliability (k_eff, density), so tau(x) -> 0 in sparse regions and -> 1 only where the local estimate is trustworthy.

Obstacle 2 - Leakage / signal collapses to global [conjectured->measured]. If reliability gating is too conservative or the locality signal degenerates (distances saturate), tau(x) -> 0 everywhere and the "field" silently becomes the global constant; you then CLAIM local adaptivity but DELIVER a global calibration, and pooled accuracy looks fine because a global calibration is a legitimate weaker method. Design answer: an ANTI-LEAKAGE test - regress the realised local correction delta_c(x) = b_c(x) - b_c^glob on the global clock and per-neighbourhood diagnostics, and require a NON-TRIVIAL residual CORRELATED with per-neighbourhood error; if residual ~ 0 or uncorrelated, the claim is falsified.

Obstacle 3 - Capacity confound (more knobs trivially help) [conjectured->measured]. A locally-varying field has strictly more capacity than a global constant, so any gain is confoundable as "more degrees of freedom fit the eval set," independent of whether topology carries signal. Design answer: a matched PLACEBO - run the FULL machinery (same number of corrections, same gates, same shrinkage) on RANDOM / SHUFFLED neighbourhoods whose marginals (k, distance distribution, label marginal) match the true ones; CONTOUR must beat Placebo or the local topology is doing nothing.

Obstacle 4 - Retrieval representation and metric are themselves uncertain [designed]. The local topology depends on the embedding h(.) and the distance (frozen sentence encoder vs LLM hidden states; cosine vs L2). Raw-space distances are anisotropic and uncalibrated, so a fixed radius means different things in different regions and a global H mis-scales density; H(x) = c0 * median_i(d_i) is itself a noisy kNN statistic. Design answer: report across at least two retrieval representations; self-tune H(x) from local distances; down-weight the kNN vote via lambda(x) where local density is unreliable.

Obstacle 5 - Verbaliser-posterior coupling (knobs must stay separable) [designed]. Both knobs write to the SAME score vector; if not kept separable, the local calibration can secretly re-encode the kNN vote (both depend on neighbourhood label mix), making "two orthogonal knobs" one knob counted twice and breaking independent ablation. Design answer: distinct functional roles - KNOB 1 acts through a CONTENT-FREE local bias (touches the LLM prior, not neighbour labels), KNOB 2 through the neighbour LABEL vote (touches labels, not the prior); each has its OWN exact collapse, and a 2x2 factorial must show additive, not redundant, contributions with correlation(b_c(x), log pi_c^kNN(x)) reported.

Obstacle 6 - Tuning-free thresholds without per-dataset tuning [designed]. The point is frozen, inference-time, no retraining; but g_cal, g_knn, k, H, tau, lambda are knobs. If they need per-dataset tuning, any gain is contaminated by oracle hyperparameter selection. Design answer: a SINGLE default configuration across all datasets and label-sets (k, c0, shrinkage prior fixed a priori; H(x), tau(x), lambda(x) are FUNCTIONS of local statistics, not free scalars); gates toggle only for ablation; any tuning is on a held-out split disjoint from test.

Obstacle 7 - Heterogeneous failure is invisible to standard metrics [conjectured->measured]. By the FLATFIELD argument, r(x) = b*(x) - b_glob has near-zero pool mean, so it CANCELS in pooled accuracy / Macro-F1 while flipping decisions in specific neighbourhoods; a naive evaluation literally CANNOT SEE the targeted effect. Design answer: STRATIFIED / CONDITIONAL metrics - accuracy and Macro-F1 as a function of neighbourhood heterogeneity (dispersion of per-neighbourhood majority-label bias), local density dispersion, and distance-to-boundary, plus ECE for the calibration knob.

Bottom line: the difficulty is the CONJUNCTION - a small-k local estimator is intrinsically high-variance (1) so it must shrink, but shrinkage risks collapsing the field to global (2); a field trivially adds capacity, so gains must be separated by a matched placebo (3); the geometry it reads is estimated, anisotropic and noisy (4); the two knobs share a score vector and must be engineered orthogonal (5); one fixed config must run across 2..77 label-sets without tuning (6); and the phenomenon is invisible to the pooled metric everyone reports (7).

**[Question 4] - Why hasn't it been solved before?**

P1 (the same global limitation, dressed differently, recurs across every prior family). Walking the prior art, the SAME structural move appears each time: a GLOBAL scalar/affine correction, or a GLOBALLY-weighted retrieval/vote. (a) The calibration lineage corrects bias with ONE global object - contextual calibration fits a single affine map (W, b) from content-free inputs [12]; domain-context and label-bias calibration estimate one corpus-level prior shift [14]; prototypical calibration fits one boundary in feature space [21]; noisy-channel and surface-form reweighting apply one global reparametrisation [20], [22]. All are input-independent: they satisfy C1 (frozen, inference-time) and can satisfy C4 (a global affine map collapses cleanly), but fail C2 and C3 because they condition on NO local signal and model NO per-neighbourhood field. (b) The retrieval / kNN-ICL lineage DOES read a local signal but spends it only to SELECT or to VOTE under a GLOBAL bandwidth - KATE retrieves nearest demonstrations [16], EPR learns a retriever [17], kNN-Prompting and kNN-LM interpolate a nearest-neighbour distribution with ONE global weight / temperature [18], [19]. These satisfy C1 and C2 but fail C3 (single global constants, not a field) and C4 (changing the prompt or the global vote is not an exact decision-for-decision collapse). (c) Verbaliser / prompt-engineering methods such as PET [9] and LM-BFF [11] change the template or fine-tune and use a global verbaliser, failing C1/C2/C3. Every family is a near-miss satisfying AT MOST TWO clauses; the recurring barrier is that the local topology, when used at all, drives RETRIEVAL or a GLOBAL vote - never a measured local correction field.

P2 (the barriers that kept the gap open). Four barriers explain the gap. (i) The calibration field treated bias as a SINGLE global affine map by design [12], [14], so its hypothesis class {constant b} cannot contain a heterogeneous optimum b*(.) when Var_x[b*(x)] > 0 - the gap is representational, not a tuning failure. (ii) Retrieval-ICL used the local signal only to SELECT demonstrations or cast a globally-weighted vote [16], [17], [18], [19], so the bandwidth and interpolation weight stayed global; nobody promoted them to a per-query field. (iii) There was no exact-collapse discipline: prior methods perturb the baseline unconditionally (new prompt, new vote, new boundary), so they are not strict safe generalisations and could not ship with a provable do-no-harm guarantee - there was no design pressure toward a zero-gate identity. (iv) The heterogeneous loss was INVISIBLE to pooled accuracy (Question 2, P2), so it was never isolated as an object of study.

P3 (how CONTOUR differs - the unclaimed C1-C4 conjunction). CONTOUR makes BOTH knobs a measured per-neighbourhood FIELD read for free from the kNN local topology: KNOB 1 a local affine calibration field b(x) shrunk toward global by a reliability-gated tau(x), and KNOB 2 a local bandwidth / kNN-evidence field with H(x) and lambda(x). The two knobs are orthogonal and independently ablatable, each with its own provable exact collapse (g_cal=0; g_knn=0), and the joint zero-gate identity gives decision-for-decision equivalence to vanilla ICL (E_collapse <= 1e-6). This is the 4-way conjunction satisfied by NO prior family: C1 frozen-LLM inference layer / no fine-tuning, C2 conditions on the explicit observable local signal, C3 models a per-neighbourhood FIELD of (bandwidth, affine calibration), C4 provable exact collapse. The boundary limits make the unification concrete: tau(x)=0 recovers global contextual calibration [12]; H->inf, lambda=1 recovers a kNN majority vote [18]; a global-constant lambda recovers kNN-LM-style interpolation [19]; H->0 recovers 1-NN top-1 retrieval [16]; and a global-prior bias recovers a PET-style label-prior correction [9] - each named method a degenerate slice of CONTOUR's field. Epistemics held exactly: boundary reductions and the zero-gate identity are [provable-by-construction]; the estimators b(x), H(x), lambda(x), tau(x) are [designed]; the accuracy improvement is [conjectured->measured], CONDITIONAL on neighbourhood heterogeneity, never asserted as a guaranteed global number.

**[Question 5] - What are the key components of my approach and results?**

Pipeline (inference-time, no fine-tuning). Everything runs with the LLM FROZEN; no gradient touches the LLM, encoder, or any parameter, and the prompt (demonstrations + query) is IDENTICAL to vanilla ICL at the same shot count - the ONLY difference is the field on the score vector. Offline (once per dataset): fix the verbaliser V; build a kNN index over the TRAIN pool ONLY (embed each train x_i with frozen h(.), store (h(x_i), y_i)); estimate the global content-free bias b_c^glob once [12]. Per query x: compute s_c(x) = aggregate_{v in V(c)} log p_LLM(v | prompt(x), x) and retrieve N_k(x) under d_i = ||h(x) - h(x_i)||. KNOB 1 (if g_cal=1): b_c^local(x) = -log p_LLM(c | contentfree, prompt_local(N_k(x))), reliability gate tau(x) from k_eff and density, shrink b_c(x) = (1 - tau(x)) b_c^glob + tau(x) b_c^local(x). KNOB 2 (if g_knn=1): H(x) = c0 * median_i(d_i), w_i = exp(-d_i / H(x)), pi_c^kNN(x) = (sum_i w_i 1[y_i=c]) / (sum_i w_i), blend lambda(x) from density reliability. Combine: f_c(x) = s_c(x) + g_cal * b_c(x) + g_knn * lambda(x) * log pi_c^kNN(x); predict argmax_c f_c(x). At g_cal=0, g_knn=0 both terms are exactly 0.0 -> f_c(x) = s_c(x) bitwise -> E_collapse <= 1e-6, CI-gated every build [provable-by-construction].

Backbones (open, frozen). GPT-2 (large/xl) for the classic ICL-classification regime; GPT-Neo 1.3B/2.7B and GPT-J 6B as mid-scale open decoders; a modern open LLM (Llama-2-7B or Llama-3-8B, and Mistral-7B [8]). All frozen; two retrieval representations for h(.) (frozen sentence encoder vs LLM last-layer hidden state) test Obstacle 4.

Datasets and verbalisers (real, named). SST-2 and SST-5 [26] (binary and 5-way sentiment, the latter stressing ordinal bias); AG News [27] (topic, C=4); TREC [28] (question type, C=6); DBpedia [27] (ontology, C=14); RTE via GLUE [29] (entailment, C=2); and a many-label stressor Banking77 [10] (C=77) or a Milios-style many-label setup [23]. Label-set sizes span 2 -> 5 -> 4 -> 6 -> 14 -> 2 -> 77, deliberately exercising the no-per-dataset-tuning requirement (Obstacle 6).

Baselines (compute- and shot-matched). B0 Vanilla verbaliser ICL = CONTOUR at (g_cal=0, g_knn=0) [provable-by-construction]; B1 global contextual calibration [12] = (g_cal=1, tau=0, g_knn=0); B2 kNN-Prompting [18] / KATE [16] retrieval-vote with global bandwidth (the kNN-Prompting limit at g_knn=1, lambda=1, H->inf; the KATE 1-NN limit at H->0); B3 kNN-LM-style global interpolation [19] at g_knn=1, lambda(x)=lambda constant. All baselines receive the SAME demonstrations, SAME k, SAME backbone, SAME compute, and a TRAIN-only index (uniform anti-leakage).

Metrics. Primary: accuracy + Macro-F1. Targeted/conditional (the ones that can SEE the effect): accuracy / Macro-F1 stratified by neighbourhood heterogeneity (dispersion of per-neighbourhood majority-label bias), by local density dispersion, and by distance-to-boundary; ECE for calibration; E_collapse for the CI gate; and compute/latency overhead of the kNN lookup as wall-clock and FLOPs delta vs vanilla.

Ablation ladder. A 2x2 knob factorial (g_cal in {0,1}) x (g_knn in {0,1}) with corners vanilla / calibration-only / bandwidth-only / full CONTOUR, plus a separability check (additivity of (1,0) and (0,1) toward (1,1); correlation(b_c(x), log pi_c^kNN(x))) for Obstacle 5. Boundary-case sanity checks recover B1, the kNN-Prompting limit, the kNN-LM limit, the 1-NN/KATE limit, and the PET label-prior limit by construction. Diagnostic rungs: ORACLE upper bound (per-neighbourhood best correction from test labels - head-room, Obstacle 7); PLACEBO (full machinery on shuffled matched-marginal neighbourhoods - capacity vs topology, Obstacle 3); RECOVERY (does the calibration knob rescue vanilla's high-bias-neighbourhood errors more than random ones, Obstacle 1/7); LEAKAGE (non-trivial residual correlated with per-neighbourhood error, Obstacle 2); plus tau-shrinkage (tau=1 naive-local vs tau(x) adaptive), retrieval-representation rows (Obstacle 4), and a single fixed-config-across-datasets row (Obstacle 6).

Expected outcomes [conjectured->measured] (targets, not guarantees). Gains CONCENTRATED in high-heterogeneity / high-density-dispersion / near-boundary neighbourhoods, with near-zero effect on homogeneous neighbourhoods; do-no-harm on homogeneous regions (accuracy change <= epsilon, MEASURED, combined with the provable zero-gate collapse); CONTOUR > Placebo (topology, not capacity); a non-trivial Leakage residual correlated with error; the calibration knob rescuing a measurable fraction of high-bias-neighbourhood errors; lower ECE; and Oracle >> CONTOUR > baselines in high-heterogeneity strata. Falsifiable predictions: if Var_x[b*(x)] ~ 0 on a dataset then CONTOUR ~ vanilla within epsilon (no gain); if CONTOUR does not beat Placebo the gain is capacity not topology; if the Leakage residual is ~ 0 or uncorrelated, the local-adaptivity claim is falsified.

Q1<->Q5 ledger. "Vanilla verbaliser ICL" -> B0 corner, shot/compute-matched [provable-by-construction]. "Two global mechanisms replaced" -> the 2x2 factorial replaces exactly KNOB1 calibration + KNOB2 bandwidth [designed]. "One fixed bandwidth -> neighbourhood-conditional bandwidth" -> KNOB 2 H(x), w_i, g_knn-only rung [designed]. "One global calibration -> affine calibration field" -> KNOB 1 b_c(x) shrunk via tau(x), g_cal-only rung [designed]. "Estimated from each query's kNN local topology" -> density via d_i/H(x), bias via prompt_local(N_k(x)) [designed]. "Improve in heterogeneous neighbourhoods" -> conditional stratified metrics, concentrated gain [conjectured->measured]. "Provably collapsing decision-for-decision" + "E_collapse <= 1e-6" -> exact-collapse corner + CI gate + boundary checks [provable-by-construction]. Implicit clauses: frozen-LLM inference-time [provable-by-construction]; gain is topology not capacity -> Placebo [conjectured->measured]; genuinely local not disguised global -> Leakage [conjectured->measured]; do-no-harm on easy regions -> homogeneous stratum <= epsilon + zero-gate collapse. Every accuracy claim is CONDITIONAL; the only unconditional claims are the provable collapse/boundary identities.

Responsible-AI considerations (Rai, advisory YELLOW): (1) Disaggregated evaluation is required, not optional - report accuracy, Macro-F1, and ECE by topic/category slice with WORST-SLICE alongside pooled numbers, since the FLATFIELD cancellation that hides the gap can also hide concentrated residual error. (2) Reliability-gated abstention - expose tau(x) and the kNN count as a low-reliability signal and defer (abstain / max-entropy) below a stated threshold rather than emit a confident over-corrected label in sparse neighbourhoods. (3) The exact-collapse identity (E_collapse <= 1e-6) is named as a publishable do-no-harm safety property and a shadow-deployment path (gates off, assert zero regression, then dial up). (4) Demonstration-pool provenance and a PII gate - prefer retrieving over embeddings rather than injecting raw neighbour text, and scrub PII before pool construction, to limit membership-inference / extraction risk on uncurated pools. (5) Dataset licensing is documented per benchmark (SST, AG News, TREC, DBpedia, RTE/GLUE, Banking77). (6) Per-query auditability - log neighbour IDs, gate values (g_cal, g_knn, tau, lambda), and pre/post-correction scores so a contested decision is inspectable. (7) Human-in-the-loop for consequential labels (moderation, toxicity, clinical/legal triage) - CONTOUR is decision-support, not a sole arbiter, because its failure modes are local and non-obvious.
